citation.description: Step 'pre-commit (all hooks, all files, gating)' has env: SKIP: no-commit-to-branch

criteria.criterion: The CI `pre-commit run --all-files` step in `.github/workflows/test.yml` skips the `no-commit-to-branch` hook (via `SKIP: no-commit-to-branch` in the step `env:`), verifiable by inspecting the step's `env:` block in the file.

citation.description: Step 'pre-commit (all hooks, all files, gating)' has env: SKIP: no-commit-to-branch

criteria.criterion: The same skip is applied to the mirrored `pre-commit` step in `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` (Verified gate), for consistency and to prevent latent breakage if that checkout ever lands HEAD on `main`.

citation.description: Hook 'no-commit-to-branch' with args [--branch, main, --branch, master] is present and unchanged

criteria.criterion: The `no-commit-to-branch` hook remains present and unchanged in `.pre-commit-config.yaml` (the local commit-time guardrail is preserved, not removed), verifiable by diff.

citation.description: Ticket comment by Joe Oakhart (author): 'Verified locally: on a branch named master the hook reports Failed/exit1 (reproduces CI); with SKIP set it reports Skipped/exit0. Full SKIP=no-commit-to-branch pre-commit run --all-files passes'. Named actor + specific observed outcomes constitute a concrete attestation.

criteria.criterion: Locally reproduce the skip working: `SKIP=no-commit-to-branch pre-commit run no-commit-to-branch --all-files` reports the hook as `Skipped`.

citation.description: test_branch_sensitive_precommit_hooks_skipped_in_ci parses .pre-commit-config.yaml for hook ids in _BRANCH_SENSITIVE_HOOKS, finds their pre-commit steps in both workflows, and asserts SKIP is set for each

criteria.criterion: DRIFT GUARD: a new test `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py::test_branch_sensitive_precommit_hooks_skipped_in_ci` fails the build unless every branch-sensitive pre-commit hook (derived from `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, currently `no-commit-to-branch`) is SKIPped in BOTH workflows' `pre-commit run --all-files` step.

citation.description: The assertion at line 198 calls _hook_is_skipped per hook per step and fails with a precise message naming the exact hook and workflow if absent — reverting the SKIP would cause this assertion to fail with the exact 'no-commit-to-branch' hook named. The RED→GREEN proof is attested in ticket comments (PS2: 'reverting the SKIP turns the guard RED naming the exact missing skip; restoring it → 4/4 green').

criteria.criterion: The guard is proven to have teeth (RED→GREEN): with the SKIP reverted the guard FAILS naming the exact missing skip; with it present all parity tests pass.

citation.description: The changed files are .github/workflows/test.yml, .github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml (YAML files, not Python source), and tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py (a test file adding a new test function). No Python source under src/rebar was modified, so lint/typecheck scope is unaffected. Ticket comment PS2 also attests 'ruff check + format clean; mypy clean'.

criteria.criterion: `make lint` and `make typecheck` remain green (change is the two workflow YAMLs plus the one parity-test file).
